old_claim.note: Animal Husbandry provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: Proteomics identifies skimmed ruminant milk and the paper links the dairy to extensive cattle and goat herding, making managed milk animals the required raw-material source.

source_shape.source_locator: Highlights and Discussion identify ruminant milk, extensive cattle and goat herding, and the resulting kefir cheese.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The analyzed product was made from milk obtained from managed ruminant herds.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Without the animal milk feedstock the specifically identified Xiaohe dairy product cannot be produced, so husbandry is not merely helpful context.

ontology_before: Managed milk animals were represented as one optional enabler of an unspecified dairy umbrella.
ontology_after: The direct proteomic claim makes herded ruminant milk a constitutive input to the Xiaohe product.
would_reject_if: The Xiaohe proteins were reidentified as a non-ruminant or non-dairy material unrelated to managed cattle or caprids.
short_reason: The scoped kefir cheese is made from milk supplied by managed ruminants.
